hero.subtitle: Local-first client with bidirectional sync to Google Calendar and iCloud. Privacy by design: your credentials never leave your machine.
features.title: Engineered for the modern professional
features.sub: Built from the ground up for those who value every second of their working day.
features.privacy.title: Privacy first
features.privacy.desc: Your calendar, your data. End-to-end encryption, no tracking, no harvesting. Zero compromises.
features.opensource.title: Open Source
features.opensource.desc: Free software under the MIT license. Inspect, contribute, audit.
features.multiplatform.title: Multi-platform
features.multiplatform.desc: Seamless sync across Windows, macOS, Linux and your iPhone via CalDAV.
features.dnd.title: Intuitive drag & drop
features.dnd.desc: Redesign your day in seconds with a fluid interface and natural physics.
showcase.title: The app in action
showcase.sub: Designed with the polish you expect from macOS, on any operating system.
download.title: Get started today
download.sub: Available for 64-bit Windows. Choose your preferred package.
download.installer.title: Standard installer
download.installer.desc: The easiest path. Includes automatic updates.
download.portable.title: Portable edition
download.portable.desc: Run directly from a folder or USB drive. No install required.
download.docs.title: Documentation
download.docs.desc: Full installation guide, Google and iCloud sync setup, and troubleshooting.
help.title: Help & setup
help.subtitle: Connect your external calendars. Real-time bidirectional sync with Google and iCloud.
help.google.title: Set up Google Calendar API
help.google.step1: Go to Google Cloud Console and create a new project called "Pro Calendar Sync".
help.google.step2: Under "APIs & Services" > "Library", enable Google Calendar API.
help.google.step3: Under "Credentials", create an OAuth client ID. Select Web Application.
help.google.step5: Copy the Client ID and Client Secret. Paste them into Settings > Accounts > Google.
help.google.warning: Make sure to add your email as a Test User in the OAuth consent screen, or publish it to production mode.
help.icloud.title: iCloud & app-specific passwords
help.icloud.intro: iCloud requires an app-specific password. Do not use your main password.
help.icloud.step1: Go to appleid.apple.com with your Apple ID.
help.icloud.step2: Under Sign-In and Security > App-Specific Passwords.
help.icloud.step3: Generate a new password for "Pro Calendar".
help.icloud.step5: Paste it into Settings > Accounts > iCloud together with your Apple ID.
help.problems.title: Common problems
help.problems.error401.title: Error 401: invalid API key
help.problems.error401.solution: Regenerate your API key and check that you enabled Calendar API in the correct project.
help.problems.sync.title: Synced events appear duplicated
help.problems.sync.solution: Clear cache from Settings > Advanced > Reset sync cache.
help.problems.timeout.title: Sync timeout
help.problems.timeout.solution: Check your network. If it persists, increase the sync interval to 15 minutes.
help.problems.denied.title: Permission denied on startup
help.problems.denied.solution: Run as administrator the first time so it can create the local database.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to Pro Calendar. By using this application you accept the terms described below.
terms.s1.title: 1. Acceptance of terms
terms.s1.body: By installing or using Pro Calendar, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the software.
terms.s2.title: 2. License
terms.s2.body: Pro Calendar is distributed under the MIT license. You can use, copy, modify and distribute the software freely, respecting the original copyright notice.
terms.s3.title: 3. Acceptable use
terms.s3.body: Do not use the application for illegal purposes, to violate third-party privacy, or to overload the external Google or Apple services it connects to.
terms.s4.title: 4. No warranty
terms.s4.body: The software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. Developers are not responsible for data loss or incorrect synchronization.
terms.s5.title: 5. Third-party services
terms.s5.body: Pro Calendar integrates with Google Calendar and iCloud. Use of these services is subject to their own terms. We are not responsible for API changes or downtime.
terms.s6.title: 6. Changes to the terms
terms.s6.body: We may update these terms. The last updated date will be shown at the start of the document.
terms.s7.title: 7. Contact
terms.s7.body: For any questions about these terms, open an issue in our GitHub repository.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Pro Calendar we value your trust. This policy describes how we collect, use and protect your personal data.
privacy.s1.title: 1. Introduction
privacy.s1.body: Pro Calendar is a local-first application. Your data lives exclusively on your device. We have no servers where your events, contacts or credentials are stored.
privacy.s2.title: 2. Data we collect
privacy.s2.account: Account info: Apple ID and/or Google OAuth client you voluntarily enter to enable sync.
privacy.s2.calendar: Calendar data: event titles, attendees, locations and times, synced directly between your device and the services you connect.
privacy.s3.title: 3. Data usage
privacy.s3.l1: Synthesize your external calendars into a unified view.
privacy.s3.l2: Show you local reminders and notifications.
privacy.s3.l3: Resolve conflicts between your different calendars.
privacy.s4.title: 4. Security
privacy.s4.body: Credentials are stored encrypted in the local database on your machine. They never leave your machine for our servers (we have none).
privacy.s5.title: 5. Your rights
privacy.s5.body: You can at any time: view, export, delete your data, or disconnect any linked account. All from Settings > Accounts.
privacy.s6.title: 6. Contact
privacy.s6.body: If you have privacy questions, open an issue in our GitHub repository.
